question_bank_items.prompt: On a standard street atlas, which direction is normally at the top of the map page?
question_bank_items.explanation: Most printed and digital maps are drawn with north at the top unless a rotation marker says otherwise.
question_bank_items.tip: Check the north arrow or page orientation before planning a route.

incorrectExplanations.South: South is normally at the bottom of a north-up map.
incorrectExplanations.East: East is normally to the right on a north-up map.
incorrectExplanations.West: West is normally to the left on a north-up map.

question_bank_items.prompt: Why should a driver check one-way streets before finalising a route?
question_bank_items.explanation: A route that looks direct on the page may be impossible or unsafe if it goes against a one-way restriction.
question_bank_items.tip: Use one-way arrows and access restrictions to confirm the route is driveable.

options: To avoid planning a route against the permitted traffic direction
To estimate the age of the road surface
To identify all bus stops on the route
To avoid using any A roads
payload.correctAnswer: To avoid planning a route against the permitted traffic direction

incorrectExplanations.To estimate the age of the road surface: Road surface age is not the purpose of one-way signs.
incorrectExplanations.To identify all bus stops on the route: Bus stops may matter for observation, but one-way checks are about permitted direction.
incorrectExplanations.To avoid using any A roads: A roads may be appropriate; one-way checks are not a ban on main roads.

question_bank_items.prompt: Click King's Cross St Pancras station area.
question_bank_items.explanation: King's Cross St Pancras is a major north London transport interchange on Euston Road.
question_bank_items.tip: Use Euston Road and the nearby rail termini as anchors.

payload.acceptedAreaDescription: The station complex and immediate entrances around King's Cross and St Pancras.

question_bank_items.explanation: Euston station sits on Euston Road west of King's Cross and St Pancras.
question_bank_items.tip: Follow Euston Road west from King's Cross to locate Euston station.

payload.acceptedAreaDescription: The Euston station concourse and immediate frontage on Euston Road.

question_bank_items.prompt: Plan a direct route from King's Cross St Pancras to Euston station.
question_bank_items.explanation: The straightforward route uses Euston Road westbound, while still requiring attention to junctions, bus lanes, and access restrictions.
question_bank_items.tip: Start by identifying the main westbound road corridor before checking local turns.

payload.title: King's Cross St Pancras to Euston station
payload.idealRouteDescription: Use the Euston Road corridor westbound from King's Cross St Pancras toward Euston station, checking access restrictions and junction positioning before the final approach.
